title: IP Addressing, DNS, DHCP, NAT, VLAN, and VPN · Spiral Incident Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs ip addressing, dns, dhcp, nat, vlan, and vpn from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered IP configuration and dependency trace after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Interpret IPv4 and IPv6 address, prefix, host, subnet, gateway, loopback, link-local, private, public, dynamic, and static roles.
Trace discovery, lease, reservation, exclusion, scope, options, renewal, relay, conflict, fallback, and authoritative ownership.
Trace local names, hosts data, client cache, configured resolver, recursive and authoritative answers, record types, TTL, search suffixes, and application caching.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vpn Evidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Duplicate addresses, stale names, a guest isolation leak, an unreachable subnet, and VPN split behavior are all blamed on DNS. The review variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate tunnel establishment, identity, certificate, routes, DNS, split policy, address overlap, filtering, application state, and privacy limits.

Inspect the Vpn Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vpn Evidence.

stimulus.title: Vpn Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Duplicate addresses, stale names, a guest isolation leak, an unreachable subnet, and VPN split behavior are all blamed on DNS. The review variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate tunnel establishment, identity, certificate, routes, DNS, split policy, address overlap, filtering, application state, and privacy limits.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A connected VPN status means all intended traffic is protected and reachable.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate tunnel establishment, identity, certificate, routes, DNS, split policy, address overlap, filtering, application state, and privacy limits. Artifact under review: layered IP configuration and dependency trace.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Vpn Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Vpn Evidence.
